text:
Databases are essential for storing and organising information. The two main types are SQL and NoSQL. SQL databases, like PostgreSQL, use structured tables with rows and columns, making them ideal for complex queries and relationships. NoSQL databases, such as MongoDB, are more flexible and store data in documents, which works well for unstructured or rapidly changing information.

Data modeling involves planning how your data will be structured before building the database. Once designed, you can run queries to retrieve specific information. Indexes are a powerful feature that speed up queries by allowing the database to find data quickly. Understanding these concepts helps you choose the right database and use it effectively.
